channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Author correction: the stard-ai reporting guideline for diagnostic accuracy studies using artificial intelligence
item.description: Nature Medicine - Author Correction: The STARD-AI reporting guideline for diagnostic accuracy studies using artificial intelligence

item.title: Big-time AI law kicking into gear requiring chatbots to tell you they are AI, but might not move the needle
item.description: The EU AI Act provision on AI disclosure kicks into gear on August 2, 2026. It's a big deal. Will it be useful or not? An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

item.title: Ranking: Who are the insurance industry’s AI talent, maturity leaders?
item.description: One out of every 50 employees working at a group of 30 large insurers is an AI specialist, according to an analysis published by a benchmarking firm, which ranks Allianz, AXA and Chubb as AI talent leaders. For London-based Evident, …

item.title: “learn to love maha” vs. “how to make the cdc great again”?
item.description: MAHA apologist Dr. Joseph Marine recently wrote an article on how to make the CDC great again. Wait, what? Trump's been in office a year and a half, and the CDC isn't great? The post “Learn to love MAHA” vs. “how to make the CDC great again”? first appeared o…

item.title: Ai-powered digital marketing: the cybersecurity blind spot every marketer must fix + video - undercode testing
item.description: AI-Powered Digital Marketing: The Cybersecurity Blind Spot Every Marketer Must Fix + Video - "Undercode Testing": Monitor hackers like a pro. Get real-time

item.title: Dietary intervention and bmi reduction in individuals at the extremes of genetic predisposition to higher bmi: a randomized controlled trial
item.description: In the GENEROOS randomized controlled trial, adults at the extremes of BMI polygenic score responded equally to a six-month dietary intervention. Genetic predisposition to obesity did not modify weight loss.

item.title: Pervasive interactions between exposures and polygenic risk can inform more effective clinical and behavioral interventions
item.description: This study examines interactions between polygenic scores and pairs of environmental contexts across seven diseases and introduces the proportion needed to benefit as a metric to quantify the expected effectiveness of interventions as a function of polygenic …

item.title: Meta admits its first ‘superintelligence’ was too stupid to survive for three days
item.description: Pulls AI-powered image tweaker after allowing free-for-all

item.title: Lenovo denies using banned chinese ssds where they're not allowed
item.description: PLUS: Canadian/Bhutanese datacenter for India; China re-uses a rocket; Australia signals AI intervention; And more!

item.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
item.description: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

item.title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
item.description: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to compete with rival services such as Apple Maps.

item.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s loo

item.title: Op-ed: canada rewrites the rules on AI privacy with bill c-36, and this is just the start
item.description: This first step is to get a grip on the realities of privacy in the Age of AI. Let’s hope it works. The post Op-Ed: Canada rewrites the rules on AI privacy with Bill C-36, and this is just the start appeared first on Digital Journal.

item.title: When dead sims died: how the ncc clean-up made nigerian telecoms more honest
item.description: By Lanre Basamta There are moments in the life of an industry when progress does not look like growth. It looks like a subtraction. It looks like pain. It looks like a sudden fall in numbers that once gave comfort to executives, investors, regulators, adverti…

item.title: It's an AI web, and we're just rats in the walls
item.description: Bots, not people, are now the ones who use the internet the most

item.title: Cardiovascular outcomes of empagliflozin-GLP-1RA combination therapy in type 2 diabetes: EMPRISE study
item.description: The EMPRISE study evaluated cardiovascular outcomes in type 2 diabetes patients receiving combination therapy with empagliflozin (an SGLT2 inhibitor) and GLP-1 receptor agonists. This pharmacoepidemiological research from Brigham and Women's Hospital examined the safety and efficacy profile of this

item.title: Formulation development and optimization of sparfloxacin-loaded solid lipid nanoparticles integrated with microneedles for the treatment of biofilm-infected wounds
item.description: Researchers developed and optimized solid lipid nanoparticles containing sparfloxacin, an antibiotic, integrated with microneedles as a delivery system for treating biofilm-infected wounds. This formulation approach combines nanoparticle technology with microneedle delivery to enhance therapeutic ef

item.title: Machine Learning-Based PAT Tool for Real-Time Residual Moisture Monitoring in Lyophilized Biopharmaceuticals Using NIR Spectroscopy
item.description: Researchers have developed a machine learning-based process analytical technology (PAT) tool that uses near-infrared (NIR) spectroscopy to monitor residual moisture in real-time during lyophilization of biopharmaceutical products. The tool integrates machine learning algorithms with NIR spectroscopi

item.title: Scientists’ side hustle? using AI and quantum computing to generate new peptides
item.description: Researchers cobbled together funding and time to show how quantum computing could aid in the development of drugs to help underserved populations and combat rare diseases.

item.title: Australia has been dragged into a us-china cage fight over AI
item.description: When it comes to AI, Australia is a spectator at a UFC cage fight between the United States and China, except the spectators are inside the cage with the combatants.

item.title: Op-ed: is it too early to appreciate AI?
item.description: While AI offers significant benefits across sectors from healthcare and retail to insurance and cybersecurity, it remains imperfect, can amplify risks such as misinformation, bias and cybercrime, and therefore deserves cautious respect rather than unquestioni…

item.title: Is the AI bubble about to burst?
item.description: Artificial Intelligence has grown in popularity over the past five years, from something consumers only knew from sci-fi movies to a widely used technology. Many companies are now using AI in some form, and it has become part of everyday life for many consume…

item.title: 7 american cities remote workers are choosing in 2026
item.description: Remote workers are looking beyond America's biggest cities. Discover seven U.S. destinations offering affordability, outdoor access, and a better quality of life.

item.title: Lorde says ray-ban meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
item.description: Lorde was performing at the Real Cool Festival in Madrid on Thursday and took some time during her set to speak out against AI glasses. While she didn't specify any brands in particular, it's likely she was taking a shot at festival sponsor Ray-Ban, which has…

item.title: Memory makers are slaves to the boom-bust rollercoaster, and the AI boom is the wildest ride of all
item.description: The RAMpocalypse may be the precursor to the AIpocalypse

item.title: Enhancing Outcome Measurement in Oncology Clinical Trials Through Artificial Intelligence: A Scoping Review
item.description: This scoping review examines the application of artificial intelligence to enhance outcome measurement in oncology clinical trials. The review synthesizes evidence on how AI can improve the accuracy, efficiency, and standardization of outcome assessment across oncology trial designs and populations.

item.title: Greece unveils national AI act framework: what changes for citizens, businesses and government
item.description: Greece's parliamentary committee has now begun debating the national bill implementing the European Union's AI Act, as Digital Governance Minister Dimitris Papastergiou stresses balancing citizen protection with innovation across healthcare, employment, and p…

item.title: On 2 august 2026, europe’s grace period on its landmark AI law ends and the commission gains the power to fine the makers of the most powerful models — for how they trained them and for what those systems do in the world
item.description: On August 2nd 2026, the European Union’s rulebook for artificial intelligence stops being a set of deadlines on paper and starts having teeth. From that day, the European Commission can supervise the companies that build the most powerful AI models, and fine…

item.title: Openai’s head of safety is leaving the company
item.description: Johannes Heidecke’s departure comes as OpenAI tries to further integrate its research and safety teams.

item.title: Calcium signaling pathway implicates a shared genetic basis between psychiatric and cardiovascular diseases
item.description: A study identifies calcium signaling pathway involvement in a shared genetic basis between psychiatric and cardiovascular diseases, suggesting common molecular mechanisms underlying both disease categories. This finding, derived from analysis of genetic data, may have implications for understanding

item.title: Digital and AI-enabled models in pharmaceutical development and manufacturing: a regulatory-focused industry survey
item.description: This industry survey examines the implementation and regulatory considerations of digital and AI-enabled models across pharmaceutical development and manufacturing processes. The study, conducted by experts from academic institutions and industry, provides insights into current practices and regulat

item.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
item.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

item.title: Leafy greens linked to 16% lower rate of copd and better lung health
item.description: Eating more leafy green vegetables such as spinach, kale, and broccoli may help keep the lungs working better with age, according to a large new study linking higher vitamin K1 intake to a lower risk of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.Continue ReadingCa…

item.title: Openai bets on families as chatgpt goes deeper into households | techcrunch
item.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

item.title: AI customers are coming around to the idea that small is beautiful
item.description: OpenAI and Anthropic have built AI Swiss Army Knives, but the future may be smaller built-for-purpose tools

item.title: Application of plant viruses in biotechnology with relation to artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology
item.description: This review examines the application of plant viruses in biotechnology, with emphasis on their integration with artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology approaches. The article explores how these emerging technologies can enhance the development and utilization of plant virus-ba

item.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
item.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

item.title: Which ‘ai scientist’ suits your lab? a guide for the perplexed
item.description: General purpose AI tools for science, such as Claude Science, promise to accelerate research. But which is right for you?

item.title: Health system learning enables generalist neuroimaging models
item.description: NeuroVFM, trained on routine health system MRI and CT scans, learns general neuroimaging representations that improve diagnosis, report generation and triage, showing how private clinical data will power safer, more accurate medical AI.

item.title: Talk python to me: #554: trustworthy AI in healthcare and longevity
item.description: You ask an AI a question and it answers with total confidence. Most of the time, a confidently wrong answer is just an annoyance. But what if the question is medical, and there's a real patient on the other end? In that world, a hallucination isn't a bug, it'…

item.title: A call to action for an equitable global genomic system
item.description: As genomics infrastructure expands, current governance will determine whether it advances global health equity or entrenches disparities, locking biased data into future clinical tools. This Comment calls for inclusive, community-centered genomic systems, bef…

item.title: Openai says GPT 5.6 is the 'preferred model' for microsoft copilot amid breakup chatter | techcrunch
item.description: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the 'preferred model' for Microsoft Copilot amid breakup chatter | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: FDA Proposes Rule to Modernize Drug Manufacturing Registration
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a proposed rule that, if finalized, would create a streamlined registration pathway for distributed manufacturing establishments that operate as a single establishment using a “hub-and-spoke” model.

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts The AI firm Anthropic has got the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on ins

item.title: Coffee break: more on american science, thomas jefferson and AI, and natural history for the ages
item.description: Part the First: Beware of Resting on the Shoulders of Atlas. Dr. Scott Atlas is a radiologist who is now ensconced in the Hoover Institution on War, Revolution, and Peace at Stanford (naturally, and in the tower, no less). One can only surmise from the gene…

item.title: AI model mania and the new chip gold rush
item.description: Just when you thought the artificial intelligence model race might slow down, it starts up again double-time. This week alone brought new models and related services from OpenAI (twice), Meta (also twice), SpaceXAI, Anthropic and a raft of Chinese companies. …

item.title: Ai-guided identification of natural ctsl inhibitors with therapeutic potential for renal injury
item.description: Author summary Computational approaches are increasingly used to speed up early-stage drug discovery, but many disease-relevant targets still lack effective inhibitors. Cathepsin L (CTSL) is an enzyme implicated in kidney injury and blocking CTSL has shown ki…

item.title: Preventive health offers new opportunities in private healthcare as patient needs develop: ihh
item.description: Preventive health offers new opportunities in private healthcare as patient needs develop: IHHbusinesstimes.com.sg

item.title: Europe torched its own rulebook to please business, and business is still unhappy
item.description: Twenty months into the EU’s campaign to slash red tape, many of the businesses that demanded it are unimpressed. Firms told Politico the “simplification” drive is too slow, too costly, and too complicated. Politico spoke to 17 companies, consultancies, and tr…

item.title: AI labels could be coming to music streaming platforms soon
item.description: The great battle to rein in the rampant spread of online deepfakes continues.
